MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DENNIS C. BIRKHIMER, Esq. (Tel: 415-257-4700) for plaintiff Sarkis Najjarian;
Atty. ALICIA L. QUEEN, Esq. of Stratman, Schwartz & Williams-Abrrego (Tel: 510-457-3440) for Defendant Jing Jin Lin.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF SARKIS NAJJARIAN's Motion For Order That Truth Of Any Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Request For Admissions, Set Two Be Deemed Admitted By Defendant Jing Jin Lin Or, In The Alternative, For Order Compelling Defendant to Serve Further Responses and For Monetary Sanctions.

Parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for parties authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted.

The Court rules as follows: Defendant served responses to request for admissions prior to the hearing. Sanctions not awarded based on global resolution of companion motions.

Defense counsel to submit a proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at sbs@sbslawsf.com (cc'ing all parties).

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Hector Contreras, CSR No. 14051, Email: hcontreras2011@gmail.com, Tel: 916-583-4732 (302/JPT)

Off Calendar May-18-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DENNIS C. BIRKHIMER, Esq. (Tel: 415-257-4700) for plaintiff Sarkis Najjarian;
Atty. ALICIA L. QUEEN, Esq. of Stratman, Schwartz & Williams-Abrrego (Tel: 510-457-3440) for Defendant Jing Jin Lin.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF SARKIS NAJJARIAN's Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set Two And For Monetary Sanctions.

Parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for parties authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted.

The Court rules as follows: Motion to compel further responses granted. All objections except attorney work product or attorney-client privilege waived. Defendant to respond fully and completely to all discovery including request for admissions.

Defense counsel to submit a proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at sbs@sbslawsf.com (cc'ing all parties).
